enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If not managed effectively, credit risk may have ramifications for a
range of other risk categories too.
enriched_requirements.object: range of other risk categories too

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks
- Credit Risk Management) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Credit Risk Management) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into effect immediately upon issuance.
enriched_requirements.object: upon issuance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Small Finance Banks' (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
enriched_requirements.object: Finance Banks' (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(1) In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires,
Explanation: Exposure shall mean all fund-based and non-fund-based
exposures.
enriched_requirements.object: non-fund-based exposures

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
3['(viia) Entity' in the context of a 'related party' prescribed in Chapter V shall mean a
'person' other than an individual and a Hindu Undivided Family (HUF).]
Note: For the purpose of assessing the effectiveness of hedge, guidance may
be taken from the applicable accounting standards and the relevant guidance
notes of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India on the matter.
enriched_requirements.object: than an individual and a Hindu Undivided Family (HUF)

conditions:
if the
offsetting exposure has the maturity / cash flow within the same accounting
4Inserted with effect from April 01
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: An exposure shall be considered as naturally hedged only if the
offsetting exposure has the maturity / cash flow within the same accounting
4Inserted with effect from April 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Credit Risk Management) - Amendment Directions, 2026 dated January 05, 2026
enriched_requirements.object: hedged only if the offsetting exposure has the maturity / cash flow within the same accounting 4Inserted with effect from A

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) 'Bank Guarantee' shall mean financial and performance guarantees issued by
banks on behalf of their clients.
enriched_requirements.object: guarantees issued by banks on behalf of their clients

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) 'Bills Purchased and Discounted' shall mean negotiable instruments that give the
holder the right to receive stated fixed sums on demand or at a fixed or
determinable future time.
enriched_requirements.object: give the holder the right to receive stated fixed sums on demand or at a fixed or determinable future time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) 'Cash credit (CC)' shall mean a facility, under which a customer is allowed an
advance up to the credit limit against the security by way of hypothecation /
pledge of goods, book debts, standing crops, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: which a customer is allowed an advance up to the credit limit against the security by way of hypothecation / pledge of goods, b

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
1[(iiia) 'Committee on lending to related parties' shall mean a committee of the Board
of the bank entrusted with sanctioning of loans to related parties.
enriched_requirements.object: the Board of the bank entrusted with sanctioning of loans to related parties

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks may
also identify any existing Committee, other than the Audit Committee, for this
purpose.
enriched_requirements.object: Committee, other than the Audit Committee, for this purpose

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iiib) 'Contract or arrangement' shall have the same meaning as specified in Section
188(1)(a) to (g) of the Companies Act, 2013.
enriched_requirements.object: as specified in Section 188(1)(a) to (g) of the Companies Act, 2013

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iiic) 'Control' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under Section 2(27) of
the Companies Act, 2013.]
1Inserted with effect from April 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Credit Risk Management) - Amendment Directions, 2026 dated January 05, 2026.
enriched_requirements.object: as assigned to it under Section 2(27) of the Companies Act, 2013

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) 'Current Account' shall mean a form of demand deposit account wherefrom
withdrawals are allowed any number of times depending upon the balance in
the account or up to a particular agreed amount and shall also be deemed to
include other deposit accounts which are neither Savings nor Term deposit
account.
enriched_requirements.object: demand deposit account wherefrom withdrawals are allowed any number of times depending upon the balance in the account or up to a parti

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(v) 'Demand loans' shall mean all loans repayable on demand (such as cash
credit, overdraft, bills purchased and discounted, etc.) and short-term loans with
maturity up to one year, whether secured or unsecured, are considered demand
loans.
enriched_requirements.object: on demand (such as cash credit, overdraft, bills purchased and discounted, etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
2['(va) Director of a bank' shall have the same meaning as defined in Explanation (b)
to Section 20 of the Banking Regulation Act 1949 and would include a nominee
director and an independent director.]
enriched_requirements.object: as defined in Explanation (b) to Section 20 of the Banking Regulation Act 1949 and would include a nominee director and an indep

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) 'Earnings before Interest and Depreciation (EBID)' shall have the same
meaning as defined for computation of Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR),
i.e., EBID = Profit After Tax + Depreciation + Interest on debt + Lease Rentals,
if any.
enriched_requirements.object: as defined for computation of Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR), i.e., EBID = Profit After Tax + Depreciation + Interest on debt + Lease Rentals

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(vii) 'Entity' in the context of 'Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure' prescribed in
Chapter-VII of these Directions shall mean a counterparty to which a bank has
exposure in any currency.
enriched_requirements.object: which a bank has exposure in any currency

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
10[A bank shall put in place a comprehensive Board approved policy on Credit Risk
Management.
enriched_requirements.object: comprehensive Board approved policy on Credit Risk Management

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall, inter alia, cover aspects related to lending to
related parties, country risk management, unhedged foreign currency exposures,
valuation of properties including empanelment of valuers, and opening of current
accounts and CC / OD accounts.
enriched_requirements.object: aspects related to lending to related parties, country risk management, unhedged foreign currency exposures, valuation of properti

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall strictly follow the credit risk management guidelines contained in the
'Guidance Note on Credit Risk Management'.
enriched_requirements.object: risk management guidelines contained in the 'Guidance Note on Credit Risk Management'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall carry out their independent and objective credit appraisal in all cases
and must not depend on credit appraisal reports prepared by outside consultants,
especially the in-house consultants of the borrowing entity.
enriched_requirements.object: and objective credit appraisal in all cases and must not depend on credit appraisal reports prepared by outside consultant

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank/lender shall carry out sensitivity tests / scenario analysis, especially for
infrastructure projects, which shall inter alia include project delays and cost
overruns.
enriched_requirements.object: / scenario analysis, especially for infrastructure projects, which shall inter alia include project delays and cost overru

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This shall aid in taking a view on viability of the project at the time of
deciding on deferment of Date of Commencement of Commercial Operations
(DCCO) / restructuring.
enriched_requirements.object: view on viability of the project at the time of deciding on deferment of Date of Commencement of Commercial Operations (DCCO) / restru

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ascertain the source and quality of equity capital brought in by the
promoters / shareholders.
enriched_requirements.object: quality of equity capital brought in by the promoters / shareholders

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, a lender shall ensure at the time of credit appraisal that debt of the
parent company is not infused as equity capital of the subsidiary / Special Purpose
Vehicle (SPV).
enriched_requirements.object: of credit appraisal that debt of the parent company is not infused as equity capital of the subsidiary / Special Purpose Vehicle (S

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall review their extant sectoral exposure limits for consumer credit and
put in place, if not already there, Board approved limits in respect of various sub-
segments under consumer credit as may be considered necessary by the Boards
as part of prudent risk management.
enriched_requirements.object: exposure limits for consumer credit and put in place

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In particular, limits shall be prescribed for all
unsecured consumer credit exposures.
enriched_requirements.object: unsecured consumer credit exposures

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The limits so fixed shall be strictly adhered
to and monitored on an ongoing basis by the Risk Management Committee.
enriched_requirements.object: and monitored on an ongoing basis by the Risk Management Committee

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall include in their loan policies / credit appraisal processes, suitable
provisions for obtaining Credit Information Reports (CIRs) from one or more Credit
Information Companies (CICs) so that the credit decisions are based on credit
information available in the system.
enriched_requirements.object: policies / credit appraisal processes, suitable provisions for obtaining Credit Information Reports (CIRs) from one or more Cred

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall be guided by DoS.CO.PPG.BC.1/11.01.005/2020-21 dated August
21, 2020 for 'Ad-hoc/ Short Review / Renewal of Credit Facilities' and
<image>
DBS.CO.PPD.BC.No.

Credit assessments carried out by a bank shall suitably factor in the possible
impact of calamities on borrowers who may be impacted by such events.
enriched_requirements.object: possible impact of calamities on borrowers who may be impacted by such events

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of Section 20(1)(b) of the BR Act, 1949, a bank is prohibited from
entering into any commitment for granting any loans or advances to or on
behalf of any of its directors, or any firm in which any of its directors is
interested as partner, manager, employee or guarantor, or any company [not
being a subsidiary of the banking company or a company registered under
Section 8 of the Companies Act, 2013, or a Government company] of which,
or the subsidiary or the holding company of which any of the directors of the
bank is a director, managing agent, manager, employee or guarantor or in
which he holds substantial interest, or any individual in respect of whom any
of its directors is a partner or guarantor.
enriched_requirements.object: commitment for granting any loans or advances to or on behalf of any of its directors, or any firm in which any of its director

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, till the director relinquishes the directorship of either the bank or
the company, the bank shall not further renew such a facility on or after its
12Deleted with effect from April 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Credit Risk Management) - Amendment Directions, 2026 dated January 05, 2026.
enriched_requirements.object: facility on or after its 12Deleted with effect from April 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Credit Risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The interest rate charged on all such loans shall
not be lower than the rate charged to the employees.
enriched_requirements.object: rate charged to the employees

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(v) Non-Fund Based (NFB) facility on behalf of a director or his/her related party,
provided that all such facilities shall be fully secured by cash collateral of
equivalent or higher value.
enriched_requirements.object: cash collateral of equivalent or higher value

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that cash collateral would not be mandatory in exposures arising on
account of derivative transactions.
enriched_requirements.object: be mandatory in exposures arising on account of derivative transactions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of Section 20A(2) of the Act ibid, any remission made in
contravention of the provisions of sub-section (1) above shall be void and
of no effect.
enriched_requirements.object: no effect

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
While granting loans and advances against shares, statutory provisions
contained in Sections 19(2) and 19(3) of the BR Act, 1949 shall be strictly
observed.

Therefore, a bank
shall not provide loans to companies for buy-back of shares / securities.
enriched_requirements.object: for buy-back of shares / securities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall have the overall responsibility of ensuring that suitable
mechanisms are put in place for implementation of the policy on lending to
related parties by the bank.
enriched_requirements.object: of ensuring that suitable mechanisms are put in place for implementation of the policy on lending to related parties b

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The credit policy (hereinafter called the policy) of a bank, as required in terms of
the extant directions, shall contain specific provisions relating to 'lending to
related parties' in accordance with the provisions of these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: to 'lending to related parties' in accordance with the provisions of these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The policy
shall prescribe, inter alia, additional safeguards to address the risks emanating
from lending to related parties.
enriched_requirements.object: safeguards to address the risks emanating from lending to related parties

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall also have specific provisions for lending to 'specified employees'
of the bank and their relatives.
enriched_requirements.object: for lending to 'specified employees' of the bank and their relatives

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the policy shall, as a part of the whistleblowing mechanism, encourage
employees to communicate confidentially and without the risk of reprisal,
legitimate concerns, if any, about irregular, unethical, or questionable loans to
related parties;
enriched_requirements.object: of the whistleblowing mechanism, encourage employees to communicate confidentially and without the risk of reprisal, legitimate concerns

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The policy shall specify aggregate limits for loans towards related parties.
enriched_requirements.object: loans towards related parties

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Within
this aggregate limit, there shall be sub-limits for loans to a single related party
16Inserted with effect from April 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Credit Risk Management) - Amendment Directions, 2026 dated January 05, 2026
<image>
and a group of related parties.
enriched_requirements.object: to a single related party 16Inserted with effect from April 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Credit

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These limits shall be within the extant prudential
exposure limits prescribed by the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: prudential exposure limits prescribed by the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provisions of paragraph 14 in Chapter IV shall also apply to grant of loans and
advances to spouse and minor/ dependent children of the directors of banks.
enriched_requirements.object: of loans and advances to spouse and minor/ dependent children of the directors of banks

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, banks may grant loan or advance to or on behalf of spouses of their
directors in cases where the spouse has his/ her own independent source of
income arising out of his/ her employment or profession and the facility so
granted is based on standard procedures and norms for assessing the
creditworthiness of the borrower.
enriched_requirements.object: to or on behalf of spouses of their directors in cases where the spouse has his/ her own independent source of income arising ou

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such facility should be extended on
commercial terms, conforming to arms-length principle.
enriched_requirements.object: terms, conforming to arms-length principle

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the restrictions placed on loans and advances by a bank to its
directors and the companies in which its directors are interested under Section
20 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, a bank shall not have any exposure
(including investments in the equity/ debt capital instruments) to its promoters
and their relatives;
enriched_requirements.object: investments in the equity/ debt capital instruments) to its promoters and their relatives

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, nothing in paragraph 42H above shall apply in cases where a
financial institution, a scheduled commercial bank, a foreign portfolio investor
or a mutual fund holds ten per cent or more of the equity share capital of the
bank as non-strategic investment and without any control of the bank.
enriched_requirements.object: a financial institution, a scheduled commercial bank, a foreign portfolio investor or a mutual fund holds ten per cent or more of

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Loans to related parties, which are not prohibited in terms of provisions of Chapter
IV or this Chapter of these Directions, or which have been permitted under
power exercised by the Reserve Bank under clause (a) of the Explanation under
sub-section 4 of Section 20 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, except (i)
credit facilities fully secured by cash or liquid securities and in accordance to
prescribed LTV and valuation norms for such securities and (ii) Interbank loans,
<image>
shall be subject to a materiality threshold as per the credit policy, which shall
not be higher than the following ceilings:
Asset Size of the bank (in ₹ crore) Materiality Threshold Ceilings
> 10,00,000 ₹25 crore
≥ 1,00,000 to up to 10,00,000 ₹10 crore
Less than 100,000 ₹5 crore
Asset size based on the last audited balance sheet.
enriched_requirements.object: of Chapter IV or this Chapter of these Directions, or which have been permitted under power exercised by the Reserve Bank under clause (a) of the Explanation under sub-section 4 of Section 20 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: For loans, materiality threshold shall apply at individual transaction level.
enriched_requirements.object: level

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Materiality thresholds may vary for different categories of loans to related parties
as per the bank's policy.
enriched_requirements.object: of loans to related parties as per the bank's policy

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All loans above the prescribed materiality threshold shall be sanctioned either by
the Board or by the 'Committee on Lending to Related Parties' of the bank.
enriched_requirements.object: the Board or by the 'Committee on Lending to Related Parties' of the bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Directors, KMP, or 'specified employees' shall recuse themselves from
deliberations and decision on loan proposals, or contracts and arrangements,
involving themselves or their related parties.
enriched_requirements.object: and decision on loan proposals, or contracts and arrangements, involving themselves or their related parties

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such recusal shall also extend to
deliberations and decisions relating to any subsequent material changes to the
terms of such loans, including one-time settlements, write-offs, waivers,
enforcement of security, implementation of resolution plans, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: and decisions relating to any subsequent material changes to the terms of such loans, including one-time settlements, wri

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place a suitable mechanism for maintaining and periodically
updating the list of all the related persons, and the related parties thereof, as
well as the loans sanctioned by the bank to such related persons and related
parties.
enriched_requirements.object: suitable mechanism for maintaining and periodically updating the list of all the related persons, and the related parties thereof, as w

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Credit facilities sanctioned to 'specified employees' and their relatives shall be
reported to the Board on an annual basis.
enriched_requirements.object: Board on an annual basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Periodic reviews shall be conducted at quarterly or shorter intervals by internal
auditors to check, inter alia, whether guidelines and procedures in relation to
loans to related parties are being adhered to or not.
enriched_requirements.object: or shorter intervals by internal auditors to check, inter alia, whether guidelines and procedures in relation to loans to re

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any deviation from the policy relating to lending to related parties and reasons
therefor shall be reported to the Audit Committee of the Board.
enriched_requirements.object: Audit Committee of the Board

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any product, entity or structure formed with the objective of circumventing these
Directions through various means, such as reciprocal lending or quid pro quo
arrangements, and identified as such by the auditors of the bank or by the
supervisory authority and investigating agencies shall always be treated as
lending to related party.
enriched_requirements.object: lending to related party

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the provisions of Chapter V on lending to related parties, listed
banks shall continue to comply with the applicable provisions of the Securities
and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure
Requirements) Regulations, 2015, as amended from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: the applicable provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regu

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks shall also adhere to instructions placed in terms of Chapter VI- Prudential
Limits on Intra-Group Transactions and Exposure of Reserve Bank of India
(Small Finance Banks - Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025 for
transactions with their group entities.
enriched_requirements.object: placed in terms of Chapter VI- Prudential Limits on Intra-Group Transactions and Exposure of Reserve Bank of India (Small

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any non-compliance with and circumvention of these Directions shall result in
supervisory and enforcement actions as deemed appropriate by the Reserve
Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: enforcement actions as deemed appropriate by the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These actions may include imposition of monetary penalty, requirement
of full provisioning, directions to conduct staff accountability exercises, forensic
audits, and restrictions or any other supervisory and enforcement actions as
deemed fit.]
<image>
enriched_requirements.object: penalty, requirement of full provisioning, directions to conduct staff accountability exercises, forensic audits, and r

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not extend finance for setting up of new units consuming /
producing the Ozone Depleting Substances (ODS).
enriched_requirements.object: up of new units consuming / producing the Ozone Depleting Substances (ODS)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
No financial assistance
shall be extended to small / medium scale units engaged in the
manufacture of the aerosol units using chlorofluorocarbons (CFC) and no
refinance would be extended to any project assisted in this sector.
enriched_requirements.object: / medium scale units engaged in the manufacture of the aerosol units using chlorofluorocarbons (CFC) and no refinance would be ex

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall formulate appropriate, well documented and clearly defined 'Country
Risk Management' policy, within the Credit Risk Management policy prescribed in
enriched_requirements.object: and clearly defined 'Country Risk Management' policy, within the Credit Risk Management policy prescribed in

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
'Country Risk Management' policy shall
address the issues of identifying, measuring, monitoring and controlling country
exposure risks.
enriched_requirements.object: identifying, measuring, monitoring and controlling country exposure risks

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall specify the responsibility and accountability of the
various levels for the country risk management decisions.
enriched_requirements.object: accountability of the various levels for the country risk management decisions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall also put
in place procedures for ensuring that necessary steps are taken in accordance
with the policy.
enriched_requirements.object: procedures for ensuring that necessary steps are taken in accordance with the policy

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall be periodically reviewed by the Board on the basis
of the experience gained.
enriched_requirements.object: the Board on the basis of the experience gained

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall institute appropriate procedures for dealing with country risk
problems.
enriched_requirements.object: dealing with country risk problems

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
They should have in place contingency plans and clear exit strategies,
which would be activated at times of crisis.
enriched_requirements.object: plans and clear exit strategies, which would be activated at times of crisis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Appropriate systems and procedures
shall be laid down with the approval of the Board to handle situations involving
significant changes in conditions in any country.
enriched_requirements.object: the approval of the Board to handle situations involving significant changes in conditions in any country

conditions:
where a bank's net funded exposure is one per cent or more of its
total assets
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the present, only in respect
of the country, where a bank's net funded exposure is one per cent or more of its
total assets, the bank is required to formulate the Country Risk Management policy
for dealing with the country risk problems.
enriched_requirements.object: Risk Management policy for dealing with the country risk problems

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Country Risk Management policy shall stipulate rigorous application of the
'Know Your Customer' (KYC) principle in international activities which shall not be
compromised by availability of collateral or shortening of maturities.
enriched_requirements.object: of collateral or shortening of maturities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Country risk
element shall be explicitly recognised while assessing the counter-party risk.
enriched_requirements.object: assessing the counter-party risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall reckon both funded and non-funded exposures from their branches
while identifying, measuring, monitoring, and controlling country risks.
enriched_requirements.object: non-funded exposures from their branches while identifying, measuring, monitoring, and controlling country risks

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall
refer to Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions,
2025 for permissible non-fund based credit facilities.
enriched_requirements.object: of India (Small Finance Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025 for permissible non-fund based credit facilities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall take into account indirect country risk.
enriched_requirements.object: country risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For example, exposures to a
domestic commercial borrower with a large economic dependence on a certain
country shall be considered as subject to indirect country risk.
enriched_requirements.object: to indirect country risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place appropriate systems to move over to internal assessment
of country risk and shall evolve sound systems for measuring and monitoring
country risk.
enriched_requirements.object: systems to move over to internal assessment of country risk and shall evolve sound systems for measuring and monitoring count

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The system shall be able to identify the full dimensions of country risk
as well as incorporating features that acknowledge the links between credit and
market risk.
enriched_requirements.object: the full dimensions of country risk as well as incorporating features that acknowledge the links between credit and market risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall use a variety of internal and external sources as a means
to measure country risk..
enriched_requirements.object: internal and external sources as a means to measure country risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, the rating accorded by a bank to any country
shall not be better than the rating of that country by an international rating agency.
enriched_requirements.object: rating of that country by an international rating agency

conditions:
where a bank's net funded exposure
is one per cent or more of its total assets
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the present, only in respect of the country, where a bank's net funded exposure
is one per cent or more of its total assets, the bank is required to undertake internal
assessment of country risk rating.
enriched_requirements.object: of country risk rating

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The frequency of periodic reviews of country risk ratings shall be at least once a
year with a provision to review the rating of specific country, based on any major
events in that country, where bank exposure is high, even before the next
periodical review of the ratings is due.
enriched_requirements.object: a year with a provision to review the rating of specific country, based on any major events in that country

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Till such time, as banks move over to internal rating systems, a bank may use the
seven-category classification followed by Export Credit Guarantee Corporation of
India Ltd.
enriched_requirements.object: followed by Export Credit Guarantee Corporation of India Ltd

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
ECGC shall provide to a bank, on request, quarterly
updates of their country classifications and shall also inform all banks in case of
any sudden major changes in country classification in the interim period.
enriched_requirements.object: on request, quarterly updates of their country classifications and shall also inform all banks in case of any sudden major changes

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall monitor their country exposures on a real time basis.
enriched_requirements.object: on a real time basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Management of country risk shall incorporate stress testing as one method to
monitor actual and potential risks.
enriched_requirements.object: one method to monitor actual and potential risks

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Stress testing shall include an assessment of
the impact of alternative outcomes to important underlying assumptions.
enriched_requirements.object: the impact of alternative outcomes to important underlying assumptions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Board shall review the country risk exposures at quarterly intervals.
enriched_requirements.object: exposures at quarterly intervals

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The review
shall include progress in establishing internal country rating systems, compliance
with the regulatory and the internal limits, results of stress tests and the exit options
available to the banks in respect of countries belonging to 'high risk & above'
categories.
enriched_requirements.object: internal country rating systems, compliance with the regulatory and the internal limits, results of stress tests and

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case, any significant deterioration takes place in respect of any
particular country risk or overall exposure, banks shall report to the Board such
developments in its next meeting, without waiting for the quarterly review by the
Board.
enriched_requirements.object: such developments in its next meeting, without waiting for the quarterly review by the Board

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Country risk management processes employed by a bank shall require adequate
internal controls that include audits or other appropriate oversight mechanisms to
ensure the integrity of the information used by senior officials in overseeing
compliance with policies and limits.
enriched_requirements.object: that include audits or other appropriate oversight mechanisms to ensure the integrity of the information used by se

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall assess and monitor the Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure
(UCFE) of entities and maintain adequate provisioning / capital for the same,
as per the instructions provided in this Chapter.
enriched_requirements.object: Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure (UCFE) of entities and maintain adequate provisioning / capital for the same, as per the ins

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Computation of UFCE
(1) A bank shall ascertain the Foreign Currency Exposure (FCE) of all entities
at least on an annual basis.
enriched_requirements.object: Exposure (FCE) of all entities at least on an annual basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall compute the FCE following the
relevant accounting standard applicable for the entity.
enriched_requirements.object: the relevant accounting standard applicable for the entity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation:
Note: For arriving at the foreign currency exposure of entities, their exposure
from all sources including foreign currency borrowings and External
Commercial Borrowings shall be taken into account.

(2) A bank shall assess the Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure (UFCE) of
entities with FCE by obtaining information on UFCE from the concerned entity.
enriched_requirements.object: Currency Exposure (UFCE) of entities with FCE by obtaining information on UFCE from the concerned entity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the information on UFCE shall be obtained from entities on a
quarterly basis based on statutory audit, internal audit, or self-declaration by
the concerned entity.
enriched_requirements.object: on a quarterly basis based on statutory audit, internal audit, or self-declaration by the concerned entity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that UFCE information shall be audited and certified by the
statutory auditors of the entity, at least on an annual basis.
enriched_requirements.object: by the statutory auditors of the entity, at least on an annual basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provisioning and Capital Requirements
(1) A bank shall determine the potential loss to an entity from UFCE using the
<image>
largest annual volatility in the USD-INR exchange rates during the last 10
years.
enriched_requirements.object: to an entity from UFCE using the <image> largest annual volatility in the USD-INR exchange rates during the last 10 years

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: A bank shall use the data published by FEDAI on largest annual
volatility of USD-INR rate over a period of last 10 years and the same shall be
used for computation of potential loss by multiplying it with UFCE.
enriched_requirements.object: by FEDAI on largest annual volatility of USD-INR rate over a period of last 10 years and the same shall be used for computation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Note: The UFCE in currencies other than USD shall be converted into USD
using the current market rates for determining the potential loss from UFCE.
enriched_requirements.object: using the current market rates for determining the potential loss from UFCE

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall determine the susceptibility of the entity to adverse exchange
rate movements by computing the ratio of the potential loss to entity from
UFCE and the entity's EBID over the last four quarters as per the latest
quarterly results certified by the statutory auditors.
enriched_requirements.object: the entity to adverse exchange rate movements by computing the ratio of the potential loss to entity from UFCE and the

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Note: (i) In cases where a bank is not in a position to obtain information on
UFCE or EBID from listed entities for the latest quarter due to restrictions on
disclosure of such information prior to finalisation of accounts, the bank shall
have the option to use data pertaining to the immediately preceding last four
quarters for computing capital and provisioning requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: use data pertaining to the immediately preceding last four quarters for computing capital and provisioning requirements

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Table 1: Incremental Provisioning and Capital Requirements for UFCE
Potential Loss /
Incremental Provisioning
Incremental Capital
EBID (per cent)
Requirement (basis points)
Requirement
Upto 15 per cent 0 0
More than 15 per cent
20bps 0
and upto 30 per cent
More than 30 per cent
40bps 0
and upto 50 per cent
More than 50 percent
60bps 0
and upto 75 per cent
25 per centage
More than 75 per cent 80bps
point increase in the risk
weight
Explanation:
Note: The incremental provisioning for UFCE shall be based on the total
exposure amount which is used for computing standard asset provisioning and
the incremental capital requirements for UFCE shall be based on the total
exposure amount which is used for computing credit risk capital requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: total exposure amount which is used for computing standard asset provisioning and the incremental capital requirements for UFCE shall

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (4) A bank shall calculate the incremental provisioning and capital
enriched_requirements.object: and capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) A bank shall incorporate the risk of UFCE of entities in their internal credit rating
system and credit risk management policies and procedures.
enriched_requirements.object: UFCE of entities in their internal credit rating system and credit risk management policies and procedures

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) A bank shall stipulate internal limits for UFCE within the overall Board approved
risk policy of the bank.
enriched_requirements.object: UFCE within the overall Board approved risk policy of the bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Consortium Lending
(1) In the case of consortium / Multiple Banking Arrangements (MBAs), the
consortium leader / bank having the largest exposure shall have the lead role
in monitoring the unhedged foreign exchange exposure of entities.
enriched_requirements.object: in monitoring the unhedged foreign exchange exposure of entities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Note: A banks shall put in place a system for information sharing and
dissemination in terms of Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks -
Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: system for information sharing and dissemination in terms of Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Transfer and Distribution of Cre

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Exemption / Relaxation
A bank shall have the option to exclude the following exposures from the
calculation of UFCE:
(1) Exposures to entities classified as sovereign, banks, and individuals;
enriched_requirements.object: exclude the following exposures from the calculation of UFCE: (1) Exposures to entities classified as sovereign, banks, and individ

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, 'sovereign' shall include domestic and foreign
sovereign as provided in Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks -
Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: sovereign as provided in Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, the RBI regulated Financial Institutions, Bank of
International Settlement (BIS), International Monetary Fund (IMF) and
eligible Multilateral Development Banks (MDBs) listed in Reserve Bank
of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy)
Directions, 2025 shall also be considered as 'bank'.
enriched_requirements.object: 'bank'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and
Provided that the bank is satisfied that such foreign currency exposures are
appropriately hedged or managed robustly by the parent.
enriched_requirements.object: that such foreign currency exposures are appropriately hedged or managed robustly by the parent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: For example, an Indian subsidiary of an MNC incorporated
outside India may have borrowed from its parent.
enriched_requirements.object: parent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that non-individual borrowers with aggregate exposure of ₹5
crore and above from banks (Scheduled Commercial Banks (excluding Regional
Rural Banks), Local Area Banks, Small Finance Banks, and Primary (Urban) Co-
operative Banks) and Financial Institutions (All India Financial Institutions and
NBFCs (including HFCs)) obtain LEI codes
Explanation: 'Exposure' for this purpose shall include all fund based and non-fund
based (credit as well as investment) exposure of banks / FIs to the borrower.
enriched_requirements.object: with aggregate exposure of ₹5 crore and above from banks (Scheduled Commercial Banks (excluding Regional Rural Ba

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Aggregate sanctioned limit or outstanding balance, whichever is higher, shall be
reckoned for the purpose.
enriched_requirements.object: purpose

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Lenders shall ascertain the position of aggregate
exposure based on information available either with them, or Central Repository
of Information on Large Credits (CRILC) database or declaration obtained from
the borrower.
enriched_requirements.object: aggregate exposure based on information available either with them, or Central Repository of Information on Large Credits (C

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Borrowers who fail to obtain LEI codes from an authorised Local Operating Unit
(LOU) shall not be sanctioned any new exposure nor shall they be granted renewal
/ enhancement of any existing exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: exposure nor shall they be granted renewal / enhancement of any existing exposure

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, Departments / Agencies of
Central and State Governments (not Public Sector Undertakings registered under
Companies Act or established as Corporation under the relevant statute) shall be
exempted from this provision.
enriched_requirements.object: provision

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall encourage large borrowers to obtain LEI for their parent entity as well
as all subsidiaries and associates.
enriched_requirements.object: obtain LEI for their parent entity as well as all subsidiaries and associates

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In India, LEI code may be obtained from Legal Entity Identifier
India Ltd (LEIIL), a subsidiary of the Clearing Corporation of India Limited (CCIL),
<image>
which has been recognised by the Reserve Bank as issuer of LEI under the
Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007 and is accredited by the GLEIF as
the Local Operating Unit (LOU) in India for issuance and management of LEI.
enriched_requirements.object: Entity Identifier India Ltd (LEIIL), a subsidiary of the Clearing Corporation of India Limited (CCIL), <image> which has been r

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
After obtaining LEI code, a bank shall also ensure that borrowers renew the codes
as per GLEIF guidelines.
enriched_requirements.object: renew the codes as per GLEIF guidelines

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall be guided by the following aspects while formulating a policy on
valuation of properties and appointment of valuers.
enriched_requirements.object: following aspects while formulating a policy on valuation of properties and appointment of valuers

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Policy for valuation of properties
(1) A bank shall have a Board approved policy in place for valuation of
properties including collaterals accepted for their exposures.
enriched_requirements.object: policy in place for valuation of properties including collaterals accepted for their exposures

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The valuation shall be done by professionally qualified independent valuers
i.e.
enriched_requirements.object: qualified independent valuers i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: the valuer shall not have a direct or indirect interest.
enriched_requirements.object: indirect interest

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall obtain minimum two Independent valuation reports for
properties valued at ₹50 crore or above.
enriched_requirements.object: valuation reports for properties valued at ₹50 crore or above

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Revaluation of bank's own properties
In addition to the above, banks may keep the following aspects in view while
formulating policy for revaluation of their own properties:
(1) As a bank is permitted to include revaluation reserves as part of Capital in
terms of Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Prudential Norms
on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025, it is necessary that revaluation
reserves represent true appreciation in the market value of the properties
and banks have in place a comprehensive policy for revaluation of fixed
assets owned by them.
enriched_requirements.object: in view while formulating policy for revaluation of their own properties: (1) As a bank is permitted to include revaluation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such a policy shall inter alia cover procedure for
identification of assets for revaluation, maintenance of separate set of
records for such assets, the frequency of revaluation, depreciation policy
for such assets, policy for sale of such revalued assets etc.
enriched_requirements.object: for identification of assets for revaluation, maintenance of separate set of records for such assets, the frequency of reva

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall
also cover the disclosure required to be made in the 'Notes on Account'
regarding the details of revaluation such as the original cost of the fixed
assets subject to revaluation and accounting treatment for appreciation /
depreciation etc.
enriched_requirements.object: required to be made in the 'Notes on Account' regarding the details of revaluation such as the original cost of the fixed as

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) As the revaluation shall reflect the change in the fair value of the fixed asset,
the frequency of revaluation shall be determined based on the observed
volatility in the prices of the assets in the past.
enriched_requirements.object: the fair value of the fixed asset, the frequency of revaluation shall be determined based on the observed volatility in the pric

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, any change in the
method of depreciation shall reflect the change in the expected pattern of
consumption of the future economic benefits of the assets.
enriched_requirements.object: the expected pattern of consumption of the future economic benefits of the assets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall
<image>
adhere to these principles meticulously while changing the frequency of
revaluation / method of depreciation for a particular class of asset and shall
make proper disclosures in this regard.
enriched_requirements.object: principles meticulously while changing the frequency of revaluation / method of depreciation for a particular class of asset a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Policy for Empanelment of Independent valuers
(1) A bank shall have a procedure for empanelment of professional valuers and
maintain a register / record of 'approved list of valuers'.
enriched_requirements.object: empanelment of professional valuers and maintain a register / record of 'approved list of valuers'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (2) A bank may prescribe a minimum qualification for empanelment of valuers.
enriched_requirements.object: for empanelment of valuers

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Different qualifications may be prescribed for different classes of assets
(e.g.
enriched_requirements.object: classes of assets (e

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
While
prescribing the qualification, the bank shall take into consideration the
qualifications prescribed under Section 34AB (Rule 8A) of the Wealth Tax
Act, 1957.
enriched_requirements.object: qualifications prescribed under Section 34AB (Rule 8A) of the Wealth Tax Act, 1957

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall also be guided by the relevant Accounting Standard issued by the
Institute of Chartered Accountants of India.
enriched_requirements.object: the relevant Accounting Standard issued by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The records maintained by the Central Registry shall be available for search by
any lender or any other person desirous of dealing with the property.
enriched_requirements.object: by any lender or any other person desirous of dealing with the property

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
It may be noted that under the provisions
of Section 23 of the SARFAESI Act, every transaction of security interest are
required to be filed with the Registry.
enriched_requirements.object: the provisions of Section 23 of the SARFAESI Act, every transaction of security interest are required to be filed with the Registr

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Current Accounts, Cash Credit Accounts (CC), and Overdraft Accounts (OD)
may all be used as transaction accounts by the customers, which raises concerns
relating to credit monitoring by the lenders.
enriched_requirements.object: transaction accounts by the customers, which raises concerns relating to credit monitoring by the lenders

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation (1): 'Banking System' for the purpose of this Chapter shall include
Commercial Banks (including Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks, and
Regional Rural Banks, but excluding Payments Banks), Urban Co-operative Banks
and Rural Co-operative Banks (State Co-operative Banks and Central Co-
operative Banks).
enriched_requirements.object: Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks, and Regional Rural Banks, but excluding Payments Banks), Urban Co-operative

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation (2): 'Exposure' for the purpose of this Chapter means the sum of all
sanctioned fund-based credit facilities and non-fund-based facilities availed by the
borrower from the banking system.
enriched_requirements.object: sanctioned fund-based credit facilities and non-fund-based facilities availed by the borrower from the banking system

conditions:
In case of customers to whom the exposure of the banking system is ₹10 crore
or more:
18Inserted with effect from April 01
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case of customers to whom the exposure of the banking system is ₹10 crore
or more:
18Inserted with effect from April 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Credit Risk Management) Amendment Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025
Explanation: 'Collection Account' for the purpose of this Chapter means a
current account or OD account used primarily for receipts of cash inflows of
the accountholder.
enriched_requirements.object: OD account used primarily for receipts of cash inflows of the accountholder

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Restricted payments / cash outflows from such account
shall be subject to the conditions outlined in paragraph 91F of these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: conditions outlined in paragraph 91F of these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
With a view to ensuring credit discipline, lenders may include additional
covenants as per their policies in their loan agreements in mutual agreement with
borrowers.
enriched_requirements.object: per their policies in their loan agreements in mutual agreement with borrowers

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may provide such cash credit facilities as per
the needs of the customer, without any restriction under this Chapter.
enriched_requirements.object: facilities as per the needs of the customer, without any restriction under this Chapter

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may maintain current account or OD account without any restriction in
case of customers where the aggregate exposure of the banking system to the
customer is less than ₹10 crore.
enriched_requirements.object: OD account without any restriction in case of customers where the aggregate exposure of the banking system to the customer

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Funds credited into a collection account shall be remitted within two working
days of receipt of such funds to a CC account, current account, or OD account
maintained with any bank in the banking system and designated by the borrower
for this purpose (hereinafter referred to as 'designated account' in this Chapter).
enriched_requirements.object: working days of receipt of such funds to a CC account, current account, or OD account maintained with any bank in the banking s

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any disbursement of overdraft limit from an OD account, which is in the nature of
a collection account, shall be through the designated account only.
enriched_requirements.object: account only

conditions: Provided that statutory dues such as taxes
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that statutory dues such as taxes, and dues, if any, to the bank
maintaining the collection account may be debited before remitting the funds.
enriched_requirements.object: taxes, and dues

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The restrictions placed in terms of paragraph 91D(1) of these Directions shall
not be applicable to the accounts mentioned below:
(1) Accounts opened as per the provisions of Foreign Exchange Management Act,
1999 (FEMA) and notifications issued thereunder, including accounts
mandated for ensuring compliance under the FEMA framework.
enriched_requirements.object: accounts mentioned below: (1) Accounts opened as per the provisions of Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (FEMA) and notificat

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that banks operating the above-mentioned exempted accounts shall
ensure that transactions in such accounts are used only for the permitted /
specified purposes.
enriched_requirements.object: such accounts are used only for the permitted / specified purposes

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Surplus funds, if any, in such accounts shall be remitted to
the designated account.
enriched_requirements.object: designated account

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of ensuring ongoing compliance with this Chapter, all banks
shall monitor accounts maintained with them on a regular basis, and in any case
at least once every half-year.
enriched_requirements.object: them on a regular basis, and in any case at least once every half-year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
then the bank shall notify the customer(s) concerned promptly, and in any case
within one month from the date of observing such ineligibility, that the account must
either be converted to a collection account or closed.
enriched_requirements.object: promptly, and in any case within one month from the date of observing such ineligibility, that the account must eithe

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The conversion or closure
process, as the case may be, shall be completed within three months of observing
such ineligibility.
enriched_requirements.object: months of observing such ineligibility

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Accounts opened in terms of these Directions shall be appropriately flagged in
the bank's core banking solution (CBS) to ensure clear identification and to
facilitate effective monitoring.
enriched_requirements.object: the bank's core banking solution (CBS) to ensure clear identification and to facilitate effective monitoring

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks maintaining multiple accounts for a borrower
shall ensure that such accounts and transactions and cashflows therein are
monitored at the borrower level as also at the account level.
enriched_requirements.object: and transactions and cashflows therein are monitored at the borrower level as also at the account level

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that an accountholder utilise their account solely for
transactions related to their authorised business or activities.
enriched_requirements.object: utilise their account solely for transactions related to their authorised business or activities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These accounts shall
not, under any circumstances, be used as pass-through channels for facilitating
third-party transactions.
enriched_requirements.object: be used as pass-through channels for facilitating third-party transactions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that entities expressly licensed or authorised by a financial sector
regulator to facilitate third-party transactions may continue to do so.
enriched_requirements.object: authorised by a financial sector regulator to facilitate third-party transactions may continue to do so

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, such
activities shall strictly be limited to the specific transactions they are authorised to
do and shall not extend beyond that scope.

Any account that has been permitted
<image>
to carry out such third-party transactions shall be appropriately flagged in the
bank's CBS to ensure clear identification and to facilitate effective monitoring.
enriched_requirements.object: the bank's CBS to ensure clear identification and to facilitate effective monitoring

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that an accountholder, who is not licensed or authorised by
the Reserve Bank to accept deposits or to provide payment services, do not
engage in such activities through accounts maintained with them.
enriched_requirements.object: who is not licensed or authorised by the Reserve Bank to accept deposits or to provide payment services, do not engage i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Robust monitoring systems shall be implemented to detect the above prohibited
usage, including mechanisms to flag accounts exhibiting unusually high
transaction volumes, frequent pass-through activities, or inconsistencies between
the accountholder's stated line of business and transactions carried out through
the account.
enriched_requirements.object: the above prohibited usage, including mechanisms to flag accounts exhibiting unusually high transaction volumes, frequent pas

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Term loans sanctioned by the bank shall preferably be remitted directly to the
intended beneficiary's account(s) or for the specified end-use, where such
beneficiary is identifiable, rather than routing the funds through the borrower's
account.]
<image>
enriched_requirements.object: to the intended beneficiary's account(s) or for the specified end-use

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
With a view to enhance credit discipline among the larger borrowers enjoying
working capital facility from the banking system, delivery of bank credit for such
borrowers shall be as under:
(1) Minimum level of 'loan component'
In respect of borrowers having aggregate fund based working capital limit (WCL),
excluding intra-day credit facilities, of ₹150 crore and above from the banking
system, the outstanding 'loan component' (Working Capital Loan) must be equal
to at least 60 per cent of the sanctioned fund based working capital limit, including
ad hoc limits and TODs.
enriched_requirements.object: Minimum level of 'loan component' In respect of borrowers having aggregate fund based working capital limit (WCL), excluding intra-da

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Hence, for such borrowers, drawings up to 60 per cent of
the total fund based working capital limits shall only be allowed from the 'loan
component'.
enriched_requirements.object: the 'loan component'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Drawings in excess of the minimum 'loan component' threshold may
be allowed in the form of cash credit / overdraft facility.
enriched_requirements.object: form of cash credit / overdraft facility

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The bifurcation of the
working capital limit into loan and cash credit / overdraft components shall be
effected after excluding the export credit limits (pre-shipment and post-shipment)
and bills limit for inland sales from the working capital limit.
enriched_requirements.object: the export credit limits (pre-shipment and post-shipment) and bills limit for inland sales from the working capital limit

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Investment by the bank
in the commercial papers issued by the borrower shall form part of the loan
component, provided the investment is sanctioned as part of the working capital
limit.
enriched_requirements.object: loan component

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The above instructions on minimum loan component shall not be applicable on the
following:
enriched_requirements.object: following:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Sharing of Working Capital Finance
Bifurcation of working capital facility into loan component and cash credit /
overdraft component shall be maintained at individual bank level in all cases,
including consortium lending.
enriched_requirements.object: bank level in all cases, including consortium lending

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Amount and tenor of the loan
<image>
The amount and tenor of the loan component may be fixed by banks in
consultation with the borrowers, subject to the tenor being not less than seven
days.
enriched_requirements.object: in consultation with the borrowers

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks may decide to split the loan component into WCLs with different
maturity periods as per the needs of the borrowers.
enriched_requirements.object: loan component into WCLs with different maturity periods as per the needs of the borrowers

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may consider rollover of the WCLs at the
request of the borrower, subject to compliance with the directions ibid.
enriched_requirements.object: WCLs at the request of the borrower

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) For credit conversion factor for undrawn portion of working capital limits
sanctioned to the aforesaid large borrowers, a bank shall refer to Reserve Bank
of India (Small Finance Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy)
Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: of India (Small Finance Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions, instructions and
guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these Directions shall continue to
remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
enriched_requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and
any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be
imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
enriched_requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being
in force.
enriched_requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
<image>
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the Reserve Bank may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the Reserve Bank shall be final and binding.

These losses may
reduce their capacity to service the loans taken from the banking system and
increase their probability of default thereby affecting the health of the banking
system.
enriched_requirements.object: service the loans taken from the banking system and increase their probability of default thereby affecting the health of the

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The aim of the framework was that the banks shall price the risk from
Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure (UFCE) as credit risk premium which may
nudge entities to hedge their foreign currency exposures in the market.
enriched_requirements.object: Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure (UFCE) as credit risk premium which may nudge entities to hedge their foreign currency exposure
